# RUNBOOK — sg-gov-newsrooms

<!-- Generated by `zeeker runbook` (zeeker 0.9.0) on 2026-07-16. -->

> **Regeneration policy:** this file is generated ONCE and then hand-maintained.
> Re-running `zeeker runbook --force` overwrites the ENTIRE file — including
> everything written into the TODO sections below. Nothing is preserved on
> regeneration. To refresh the auto-generated facts without losing hand-written
> content, generate a fresh copy elsewhere (`zeeker runbook --output RUNBOOK.new.md`)
> and merge manually.

This runbook is the operational document for humans and AI monitoring agents:
how to run builds, what healthy output looks like, and how to interpret
failures and skips.

## Project facts (auto-generated)

- **Project:** `sg-gov-newsrooms`
- **Database file:** `sg-gov-newsrooms.db` (Datasette database name: `sg-gov-newsrooms`)
- **Title:** SG Government Newsrooms
- **Description:** Non-commercial catalogue of news releases, speeches, parliamentary replies, and announcements drawn from the official newsrooms of eight Singapore Government bodies: the Ministry of Law, the Singapore Judiciary, the Attorney-General's Chambers, the Intellectual Property Office of Singapore, the Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore, the Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority, the Ministry of Manpower, and the Personal Data Protection Commission. One table per agency; each row carries the item's metadata, a project-generated AI summary, and a link back to the original page. Full article text is extracted for indexing and summarisation only; access to it is not provided through this catalogue — read each item at its source_url. Coverage starts at items published on or after 1 January 2026, collected by daily incremental scrapes (new rows only — published items are never re-fetched or updated). Copyright in each item belongs to the Government of Singapore or the respective agency, and all rights are reserved by them; each source site's terms of use govern reuse and are linked in the corresponding table description. This project is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or published on behalf of, in collaboration with, or in partnership with any of these agencies. Summaries are AI-generated by this project, not by the agencies, and text extraction is automated and may be imperfect — always check the source agency's website (each row's source_url) for the latest and authoritative version.
- **Source:** Official newsrooms of eight Singapore Government ministries, organs of state, and statutory boards
- **License:** © Government of Singapore and the respective source agencies (Ministry of Law, Singapore Judiciary, Attorney-General's Chambers, IPOS, CCCS, ACRA, Ministry of Manpower, PDPC). All rights reserved; reuse is governed by each source site's own terms of use, linked in each table's description.

### Resources

| Resource | Description | Fragments | Fragments on skip | FTS fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `mlaw_news` | Ministry of Law news: press releases, speeches, parliamentary speeches, replies, ministerial visits, and announcements, from 1 January 2026 onwards. Discovered from the live Atom feed at https://www.mlaw.gov.sg/feed.xml, which carries roughly the 10 most recent posts and also supplies each article's body — full text is extracted from the feed entry itself, not from a detail-page scrape, so coverage depends on frequent polling. Rows are insert-only; extraction is automated and may be imperfect — the page at each row's source_url is authoritative. Source: Ministry of Law newsroom, https://www.mlaw.gov.sg/news/. © Ministry of Law. Terms of use: https://www.mlaw.gov.sg/terms-of-use/. | no | no | `title`, `summary` |
| `judiciary_news` | Singapore Judiciary news: speeches, media releases, media advisories, and announcements across the Supreme Court, State Courts, Family Justice Courts, and related bodies, from 1 January 2026 onwards. Discovered via the newsroom's Sitefinity GetFilteredList endpoint (POST, paginated per year, newest first; pagination stops once a whole page of items is already in the database), with full text scraped from each item's detail page. Rows are insert-only; extraction is automated and may be imperfect — the page at each row's source_url is authoritative. Source: Singapore Judiciary newsroom, https://www.judiciary.gov.sg/news-and-resources/news. © Government of Singapore. Terms of use: https://www.judiciary.gov.sg/terms-of-use. | no | no | `title`, `summary` |

Rows are insert-only; extraction is automated and may be imperfect — the page at each row's source_url is authoritative. Rows are insert-only; extraction is automated and may be imperfect — the page at each row's source_url is authoritative. Terms of use: https://www.ipos.gov.sg/terms-of-use. | no | no | `title`, `summary` |

Discovered from a single fetch of the announcements-and-media-releases listing page, whose Isomer React Server Component payload carries every item's path, date, title, and category label in one response (no pagination); full text is then scraped from each detail page. Rows are insert-only; extraction is automated and may be imperfect — the page at each row's source_url is authoritative. ## Command reference (auto-generated)

```bash
uv run zeeker build                        # build all resources
uv run zeeker build <resource> [...]       # selective build
uv run zeeker build --sync-from-s3         # incremental: download existing DB from S3 first
uv run zeeker build --setup-fts            # (re)create FTS indexes on configured fields (idempotent)
uv run zeeker build --json                 # machine-readable BuildReport JSON on stdout
uv run zeeker build --progress-file p.json # atomic JSON snapshot after each resource (for watchers)
uv run zeeker build --fail-on-blocked      # exit 1 if any resource skipped with kind "blocked"
uv run zeeker deploy                       # upload the built .db to S3
```

## Status contract (auto-generated — zeeker 0.9.0)

This section is baked in verbatim from the installed zeeker version. Monitors
should parse builds against this contract, not against ad-hoc log scraping.

### Exit codes (`zeeker build`)

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `0` | All resources succeeded (skips are not failures). |
| `1` | One or more resources failed, FTS setup failed, post-hook exited non-zero, `--fail-on-empty` with a skipped resource, or `--fail-on-blocked` with a resource that skipped with kind `blocked`. |
| `2` | Fatal error: schema conflict, DB open failure, config error, local-diverged sync. No coherent build ran. |

### Non-TTY line grammar (plain text output, what agents see)

One status line per resource, streamed as each finishes:

```
[OK  ] <resource>  <N> records  (<T>s)
[FAIL] <resource>  <error message>  (<T>s)
[SKIP] <resource>  <reason> (<kind>)  (<T>s)
[SKIP] <resource>  no data returned  (<T>s)
WARN[<resource>]: <message>
WARN[build]: <message>
SUMMARY: <S> succeeded, <F> failed, <K> skipped in <T>s | <k1>=<v1> <k2>=<v2>
FTS_ERROR: <message>
POST_HOOK: <command> exit=<code>
FATAL: <message>
```

- `[SKIP] name  <reason> (kind)` — the resource raised `Skip(reason, kind=...)`;
  the reason says WHY. `[SKIP] name  no data returned` is a plain `return []`
  skip (implicit kind `up_to_date`, no reason).
- Enrichment counters (see below) are appended to `[OK]`/`[SKIP]` lines as
  `; key=value key=value` (never to `[FAIL]` lines), e.g.
  `[SKIP] judgments  no data returned; updated=50 enriched=25  (12.3s)`.
- `WARN[<resource>]:` lines follow that resource's status line; `WARN[build]:`
  lines are build-level warnings (e.g. S3 sync issues).
- The `SUMMARY:` footer aggregates `extra_counts` across all resources after
  a ` | ` separator (omitted when there are no counters).

### Skip kinds and what each means for a monitor

| Kind | Meaning | Bumps `_zeeker_updates.last_updated`? | Monitor action |
|---|---|---|---|
| `up_to_date` | Source was checked; nothing new. The healthy steady state. | Yes | None. |
| `blocked` | A precondition failed (proxy down, missing env var) — the source was NOT checked. | No (time-based incremental resources stay safe) | Actionable: investigate the precondition. Use `--fail-on-blocked` to turn this into exit 1. |
| `disabled` | Intentionally switched off (feature flag / config). | No | None — not an incident. |

### Enrichment counters (`__zeeker_report__`)

Multi-phase resources that UPDATE existing rows (0 records inserted) report
work via a module-level dict in the resource module:

```python
def fetch_data(existing_table):
    global __zeeker_report__
    __zeeker_report__ = {"updated": 50, "enriched": 25, "notes": "phase2 drained 25"}
    return []
```

- Read and cleared on EVERY exit path — success, skip, even a fetch_data crash
  (work done before the crash stays visible).
- Re-read after the fragments phase; counters set in `fetch_fragments_data`
  merge in, summing on key collision.
- Int values only (other value types are ignored, never fail the build).
- Rendered as `key=value` on status lines, aggregated totals in the `SUMMARY:`
  footer, and carried as `extra_counts` / `notes` in `--json`.
- A "0 succeeded" SUMMARY with non-zero counters means real enrichment work
  happened — do not read it as a dead build.

### JSON payload (`--json` and `--progress-file` share the same schema)

Top-level fields:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | string | `success` \| `partial_failure` \| `failed` \| `fatal` |
| `total_duration_s` | float | Whole-build wall time. |
| `resources` | array | One object per resource (fields below). |
| `build_warnings` | array of strings | Build-level warnings (`WARN[build]:` lines). |
| `fts_error` | string or null | FTS setup failure, if any. |
| `fatal_error` | string or null | Set when the build died before/without a per-resource report. |
| `post_hook` | object or null | `{command, exit_code, stdout, stderr}` when `--post-hook` ran. |

Per-resource object fields:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | Resource name. |
| `status` | string | `success` \| `failed` \| `skipped` |
| `records` | int | New main-table rows inserted. |
| `duration_s` | float | Per-resource wall time. |
| `error_message` | string or null | Set on failure. |
| `traceback` | string or null | Full Python traceback on failure (paths CWD-relativized). |
| `fragments_records` | int or null | Fragment rows inserted, when a fragments phase ran. |
| `skip_reason` | string or null | The `Skip(...)` reason; null for plain returned-`[]` skips. |
| `skip_kind` | string or null | `up_to_date` \| `blocked` \| `disabled` (only meaningful when skipped). |
| `warnings` | array of strings | Per-resource warnings (`WARN[<resource>]:` lines). |
| `extra_counts` | object | Int counters from `__zeeker_report__`. |
| `notes` | string or null | Free-text note from `__zeeker_report__`. |

---

## What happens during a run

All 8 resources are single-pass, insert-only scrapers with the same three-phase
shape inside one `fetch_data()` call (zeeker 0.9.0 runs `fetch_data` exactly
once per resource per build — no module reload, no second call):

1. **Discovery** — enumerate candidate article URLs and drop those already in
   the table (dedup key: `source_url`). Discovery mechanisms differ per
   resource (see table below).
2. **Per-article scrape** — fetch each new detail page (1.5s ± 0.5s polite
   delay, 3 tenacity retries with exponential backoff per request), extract
   title/date/category/full text. In-loop skips (published before 2026-01-01,
   missing title) increment the `skipped` counter; per-article fetch failures
   increment `failed` and feed a 5-consecutive-failure circuit breaker.
3. **AI summaries** — one ~100-word summary per new article via an
   OpenAI-compatible LLM (`LLM_BASE_URL`, semaphore-bounded to 3 concurrent).
   A failed/unconfigured LLM call degrades to `summary = ""` — never fails
   the build. `mlaw_news` additionally backfills empty summaries on existing
   rows at the start of its run.

There are no fragments tables, no checkpoint files, and no on-disk caches in
this project — each run is stateless apart from the database itself.

| Resource | Discovery mechanism | Proxy? |
|---|---|---|
| `mlaw_news` | Atom feed `https://www.mlaw.gov.sg/feed.xml` (~10 most recent posts; body text comes from the feed itself, no detail fetch) | No |
| `judiciary_news` | Sitefinity `GetFilteredList` AJAX endpoint (POST, per-year, paginated) | No |
| `agc_news` | `sitemap.xml` filtered to `/newsroom/*` + Isomer listing-page RSC dates | No |
| `ipos_news` | Single listing-page fetch; items parsed from Isomer RSC payload | No |
| `ccs_news` | Single listing-page fetch; items parsed from Isomer RSC payload | No |
| `acra_news` | `sitemap.xml` filtered to `/news-events/news-announcements/*` + Isomer listing dates | No |
| `mom_news` | `newsroom.xml` sitemap, URL pattern `/newsroom/press-releases/YYYY/MMDD-slug` (year gates the 2026 cutoff pre-fetch) | No |
| `pdpc_news` | `sitemap.xml` filtered to `/media-events/*`, slug-year pre-filter, RSC body extraction | **Yes — `TAILSCALE_PROXY`** |

### Log and status-line contract (post zeeker 0.9.0 adoption)

Every log line is prefixed with the resource name; errors and degradation
warnings go to stderr. Each resource ends its run in exactly one of:

- **Healthy (any yield, including zero):**
  `{resource}: done — {new} new, {skipped} skipped, {failed} failed` (stdout),
  then zeeker prints `[OK  ] ...` (new > 0) or
  `[SKIP] {resource}  no data returned` (kind `up_to_date`). Nonzero
  `skipped`/`failed` counters are reported via `__zeeker_report__` and appear
  on the status line as `; skipped=N failed=M` and in the `SUMMARY:` footer.
- **Discovery failure:** `{resource}: ABORTED (discovery failed: {Exc}: ...)`
  (stderr; pdpc says `sitemap fetch failed`), then the resource raises
  `Skip(reason, kind="blocked")` → `[SKIP] {resource}  {reason} (blocked)`.
- **Circuit breaker, zero rows collected:**
  `{resource}: circuit breaker tripped — 5 consecutive failures (last: ...)` +
  `ABORTED (circuit breaker: ...)` (stderr), then `Skip(..., kind="blocked")`.
- **Circuit breaker, partial rows collected:** same ABORTED stderr line, but
  the partial list IS returned and inserted → `[OK  ]` with a `failed=` counter.
  Partial-abort rows are real; do not treat that OK as fully healthy — check
  the `failed=` counter.
- **pdpc_news proxy fast-skip:**
  `pdpc_news: SKIPPED (blocked: TAILSCALE_PROXY unset — proxy required for PDPC CloudFront)`
  (stderr, sub-second), then
  `Skip("TAILSCALE_PROXY unset — proxy required for PDPC CloudFront", kind="blocked")`.

`blocked` skips do **not** advance `_zeeker_updates.last_updated`, so a
proxy/site outage never masquerades as a covered window. No resource in this
repo uses kind `disabled`.

## Environment variables

Zeeker auto-loads `.env` from the project root for `build`/`deploy`. GitHub
Actions is disabled for this repo — builds run host-side, so env comes from
the host trigger's environment / `.env`.

| Variable | Needed by | Unset behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| `TAILSCALE_PROXY` | `pdpc_news` only (SOCKS5 URL, e.g. `socks5h://172.17.0.1:1055`) | pdpc_news fast-skips sub-second with `Skip(kind="blocked")` — no doomed retries. Set but exit node down → `ABORTED (sitemap fetch failed: RetryError...)` after ~20–200s, also `blocked`. |
| `LLM_BASE_URL` | All 8 (AI summaries; production: Gemma4 26B on Ollama via Tailscale) | Summaries skipped per-article with a stderr warning; rows stored with `summary = ""`. Build still succeeds. |
| `LLM_API_KEY` | All 8 | Falls back to `"not-needed"` (fine for Ollama). |
| `LLM_MODEL` | All 8 | Model name passed to the endpoint (e.g. `gemma4:26b`). |
| `S3_BUCKET`, `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, `S3_ENDPOINT_URL` | `zeeker deploy` / `--sync-from-s3` only | Build works without them; deploy fails. |

No other env vars are read. Discovery and scraping for the 7 non-PDPC
resources need no credentials or proxy at all.

## Cadence & expected yield

- **Schedule:** daily at 11:00 SGT, host-side trigger:
  `bash /workspace/group/bin/build-zeeker sg-gov-newsrooms-zeeker [resource]`.
- **Combined yield:** 0–5 new articles per day; the most common day is all 8
  resources ending `[SKIP] ... no data returned` (`up_to_date`) — that is
  healthy, not an incident.

| Resource | Typical yield | Row count (Jul 2026) |
|---|---|---|
| `mlaw_news` | ~2–5 new/week | ~50 |
| `judiciary_news` | ~3–10 new/week | ~34 |
| `agc_news` | ~1–3 new/week | ~295 |
| `ipos_news` | ~1–3 new/week | ~12 |
| `ccs_news` | ~1–3 new/week | ~16 |
| `acra_news` | ~2–5 new/week | ~367 |
| `mom_news` | ~2–5 new/week | ~64 |
| `pdpc_news` | ~1–2 new/month | ~16 |

**Durations:** 5–30s when everything is up to date. Each new article adds
~2s (polite delay) plus LLM summary time (a few seconds each against local
Ollama). pdpc_news with the proxy unset is now sub-second (`blocked` skip);
with the proxy set but the exit node down, expect 20–200s of retry backoff
before the `blocked` skip.

**Anomalies worth flagging:** any `blocked` skip on the 7 no-proxy resources
(their public sites are normally always reachable); repeated `blocked` on
pdpc_news across builds (exit node down); an `[OK]` line carrying a `failed=`
counter (partial breaker abort or flaky detail pages); total build time > 5
minutes; several days with 0 new rows across all 8 tables on weekdays.

## Failure modes & recovery

There are no checkpoint files, quarantine lists, or on-disk caches to manage —
recovery is always "fix the precondition and re-run the build". The database's
`source_url` dedup makes re-runs idempotent.

| Failure mode | Signal | Recovery |
|---|---|---|
| pdpc proxy env unset | `[SKIP] pdpc_news  TAILSCALE_PROXY unset — proxy required for PDPC CloudFront (blocked)`, sub-second | Set `TAILSCALE_PROXY` in the build environment. |
| Tailscale exit node down | `pdpc_news: ABORTED (sitemap fetch failed: RetryError[ProxyError]...)` → `blocked` skip, 20–200s | Bring the exit node (ASUS router / Mac sidecar) back online; re-run. Nothing was marked as covered. |
| Source site outage / endpoint change | `{resource}: ABORTED (discovery failed: {Exc}: ...)` → `blocked` skip | Transient: next daily run recovers. Persistent: the site's markup/endpoint changed — update the resource module. |
| Circuit breaker (5 consecutive detail-page failures) | `circuit breaker tripped — 5 consecutive failures (last: ...)` + `ABORTED (circuit breaker: ...)`. Zero rows → `blocked` skip; partial rows → `[OK]` with `failed=` counter | Transient server strain: articles missed this run are re-discovered next run (they're not in the DB yet). Repeated: investigate the last exception in the breaker line. |
| LLM endpoint down/unset | stderr `Summary failed: ...` or `LLM_BASE_URL not set — skipping summary`; rows land with empty `summary` | Fix Ollama/endpoint. `mlaw_news` backfills its own empty summaries automatically on later runs; other tables keep empty summaries (rows are insert-only — regenerate manually if it matters). |
| Per-article fetch failures below the breaker threshold | `failed=` counter on the status line; `judiciary_news` still inserts the row with empty `content_text`, other resources drop the article (re-tried next run since it never entered the DB) | Usually self-heals next run. |

Retry policy per request: 3 tenacity attempts, exponential backoff (min 1s,
max 10s). Skip is raised outside all retry decorators, so it propagates
immediately and is never retried.

## Backlog / progress queries

This project has no enrichment backlog (everything happens in one pass), so
"progress" means new rows and summary coverage.

```sql
-- Rows per table + freshest item (run against sg-gov-newsrooms.db)
SELECT 'mlaw_news' AS t, COUNT(*) AS rows, MAX(published_date) AS newest FROM mlaw_news
UNION ALL SELECT 'judiciary_news', COUNT(*), MAX(published_date) FROM judiciary_news
UNION ALL SELECT 'agc_news', COUNT(*), MAX(published_date) FROM agc_news
UNION ALL SELECT 'ipos_news', COUNT(*), MAX(published_date) FROM ipos_news
UNION ALL SELECT 'ccs_news', COUNT(*), MAX(published_date) FROM ccs_news
UNION ALL SELECT 'acra_news', COUNT(*), MAX(published_date) FROM acra_news
UNION ALL SELECT 'mom_news', COUNT(*), MAX(published_date) FROM mom_news
UNION ALL SELECT 'pdpc_news', COUNT(*), MAX(published_date) FROM pdpc_news;

-- Summary backlog (LLM was down/unset when these rows were imported;
-- only mlaw_news backfills automatically)
SELECT 'agc_news' AS t, COUNT(*) AS missing_summary FROM agc_news WHERE summary = ''
UNION ALL SELECT 'mlaw_news', COUNT(*) FROM mlaw_news WHERE summary = '';
-- (repeat for the other tables as needed)

-- Rows imported by the most recent run(s)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM agc_news WHERE created_at >= datetime('now', '-1 day');

-- Freshness markers zeeker maintains (blocked skips do NOT bump these)
SELECT * FROM _zeeker_updates;
```

To verify a build actually ran, prefer the `--json` BuildReport (or the
`SUMMARY:` line): per-resource `status`, `records`, `skip_kind`, and
`extra_counts` (`skipped=` / `failed=`) are all machine-readable there.

## Escalation

This is a personally operated project (owner: houfu) with a host-side daily
trigger — there is no pager or dashboard. Guidance for a monitoring agent:

- **Do not escalate:** `up_to_date` skips (any number), a single day of
  pdpc_news `blocked`, empty-summary rows while the LLM host is known to be
  down.
- **Surface to the owner:** the same resource `blocked` or `FAIL` on 3+
  consecutive daily builds (site or endpoint likely changed — the resource
  module needs code changes); pdpc_news `blocked` for a week (Tailscale exit
  node needs attention); any exit code `2` (schema conflict / DB corruption —
  do not retry blindly).
- **Useful levers before escalating:** re-run a single resource
  (`uv run zeeker build <resource>`), and check the two infrastructure
  dependencies — the Tailscale exit node (pdpc_news) and the Ollama host
  (summaries) — before suspecting the scrapers.
